pattern: A substring stays semi-repetitive as long as it holds at most one place where two neighboring characters match, so the longest one is found by sliding a window that remembers where its one allowed match currently sits.
hints: Scan left to right while keeping a running window; the only thing that should ever force the window to shrink is finding a SECOND place inside it where two neighbors are equal.

Remember the index of the most recent adjacent-equal pair you've seen. If you meet another one while that earlier pair is still inside your window, jump the window's left edge to just past the earlier pair.
Track the window's length after every step and keep the running maximum -- you never need to shrink the window for any other reason than a second adjacent-equal pair appearing.

edge_inputs.description: one adjacent-equal pair in the middle
all characters identical, many adjacent-equal pairs force heavy shrinking
shortest string with exactly one pair
max-length string with no adjacent-equal pairs at all
three in a row creates two overlapping pairs, must shrink past the first
several separated single pairs interspersed with distinct digits

stress.description: random digit strings up to the max length
